description_long: This is a portable record player with a retro suitcase design, featuring a yellow base and a blue lid. The lid is propped open, displaying a vibrant poster with the word 'Ukraine' and an artistic portrait. The turntable platter is white and prominently displays a 'Stand With Ukraine' graphic, with a map silhouette in blue and yellow. The control panel is black, with visible tonearm and control switches, and the corners of the case are reinforced with black protective caps. The exterior appears to be made of textured faux leather, while the interior houses the mechanical and electronic components typical of a record player.
description: A yellow and blue suitcase-style record player with a white platter featuring a 'Stand With Ukraine' graphic and a colorful poster in the open lid.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open lid with a Ukraine-themed poster, the white platter with a graphic, and the control panel with tonearm and switches.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the depth of the blue lid, the yellow base, and the side profile of the platter and control area.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the blue textured lid, black corner protectors, and the latch at the top center.
description_view_3: Opposite angled view showing the open lid, the white platter, and the control panel from the other side.
attribute_turntable_platter: Present and prominently visible, the turntable platter is circular, white, and occupies a large portion of the device's top surface. It features a 'Stand With Ukraine' graphic with a blue and yellow map and text. The surface appears smooth and matte, with no visible grooves or patterns beyond the printed design.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, black, and positioned to the right of the platter. It is straight, of moderate length, and appears to be made of plastic or metal with a matte finish. The tonearm is resting in its holder, not currently over the platter.
attribute_control_knobs: Several control knobs and switches are visible on the black control panel to the right of the platter. They are small, cylindrical, and black, likely made of plastic, with no visible labels or markings from these views.
